title: Spec List
description: Lists all specs across configured workspaces with optional summaries and metadata freshness status. Use this to audit spec coverage, identify stale metadata, or quickly navigate project capabilities.

rules: The canonical command is `specd specs list`. The alias `specd spec list` MUST behave identically.
The command accepts --format text|json|toon with text as default.
The command accepts --summary to include a summary column.
The command accepts --metadata-status to show freshness status.
The command accepts --status <status> to filter by metadata status (fresh, stale, missing, invalid).
The --workspace flag is repeatable and filters specs to the named workspace(s).
When a workspace name does not match any configured workspace, it is silently ignored with no error or warning.
In text mode, only matching workspace groups are rendered.
In JSON mode, all workspaces are present with empty specs arrays for filtered-out workspaces.
Title is first resolved from the spec's .specd-metadata.yaml title field.
If title is missing or whitespace-only, fallback to the last path segment of the spec identifier.
Summary is first resolved from the spec's .specd-metadata.yaml description field.
If description is missing, extract the first non-empty paragraph after the H1 heading in spec.md.
If no paragraph after H1, extract from the first paragraph of ## Overview, ## Summary, or ## Purpose sections.
If no summary source exists, omit the summary field entirely.
Status is fresh when all contentHashes match the current file contents.
Status is stale when any hash differs or contentHashes is absent.
Status is missing when .specd-metadata.yaml does not exist.
Status is invalid when metadata fails structural validation.
Text mode renders each workspace as a group with a heading, then a table of specs with PATH and TITLE columns.
Results are sorted lexicographically by spec path within each workspace.
When no specs exist in a workspace, render the heading followed by '(none)'.
JSON mode outputs an array of workspace objects with name and specs arrays.

constraints: No filtering by prefix in v1.

scenarios.name: Canonical plural command
when: specd specs list --format json is run
then: the command executes using the canonical plural group

scenarios.name: Singular alias command
when: specd spec list --format json is run
then: it behaves as an alias of specd specs list --format json

scenarios.name: Single workspace filter in text mode
given: the project has workspace default with specs and workspace billing with specs
when: specd spec list --workspace default is run
then: only workspace default group is rendered
AND workspace billing does not appear

scenarios.name: Multiple workspace filters
given: the project has workspaces default, billing, and core
when: specd spec list --workspace default --workspace billing is run
then: workspace groups for default and billing are rendered
AND workspace core does not appear

scenarios.name: Workspace filter with JSON output
given: the project has workspaces default and billing
when: specd spec list --workspace default --format json is run
then: the JSON workspaces array includes default with its specs and billing with an empty specs array

scenarios.name: Non-existent workspace name produces no output
given: no workspace named nonexistent is configured
when: specd spec list --workspace nonexistent is run
then: no workspace group is rendered
AND no error is raised

scenarios.name: Title from metadata
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml with title: Login Flow
then: the row for default:auth/login shows Login Flow as its title

scenarios.name: Title fallback to last path segment
given: default:auth/login has no .specd-metadata.yaml or its title field is absent
then: the row for default:auth/login shows login as its title

scenarios.name: Summary from metadata description
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml with description: Handles login
then: the row for default:auth/login shows Handles login as its summary

scenarios.name: Summary fallback — paragraph after H1
given:
default:auth/login has no metadata description but spec.md begins with # Auth Login

Handles the login flow.

## Overview
then: the row for default:auth/login shows Handles the login flow. as its summary

scenarios.name: Summary fallback — Overview section
given: default:auth/login has no metadata description and spec.md has no paragraph after the H1, but has ## Overview\n\nManages authentication.
then: the row for default:auth/login shows Manages authentication. as its summary

scenarios.name: No summary available
given: default:auth/login has no metadata description and spec.md has no extractable summary
then: the row for default:auth/login shows the title only, with no summary text
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: Summary not shown without flag
given: default:auth/login has metadata with a description
when: specd spec list is run without --summary
then: the row for default:auth/login does not include any summary text

scenarios.name: Status fresh when all hashes match
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml with contentHashes whose SHA-256 values match the current spec.md and verify.md files
then: the row for default:auth/login shows fresh as its status

scenarios.name: Status stale when hash differs
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml with a contentHashes entry for spec.md whose recorded hash does not match the current file content
then: the row for default:auth/login shows stale as its status

scenarios.name: Status missing when no metadata file
given: default:auth/login has no .specd-metadata.yaml file
then: the row for default:auth/login shows missing as its status

scenarios.name: Status stale when contentHashes absent
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml but no contentHashes field
then: the row for default:auth/login shows stale as its status

scenarios.name: Status invalid when metadata fails structural validation
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml with keywords: [123] (invalid type per strict schema)
then: the row for default:auth/login shows invalid as its status

scenarios.name: Status not shown without flag
given: default:auth/login has .specd-metadata.yaml
when: specd spec list is run without --metadata-status
then: the row for default:auth/login does not include any status text

scenarios.name: Filter by single status
given: the project has three specs: one fresh, one stale, one missing
when: specd spec list --metadata-status stale is run
then: only the spec with stale status is shown

scenarios.name: Filter by multiple statuses
given: the project has three specs: one fresh, one stale, one missing
when: specd spec list --metadata-status stale,missing is run
then: the stale and missing specs are shown
AND the fresh spec is not shown

scenarios.name: JSON output with status
given: workspace default has spec auth/login with fresh metadata
when: specd spec list --metadata-status --format json is run
then: the entry for default:auth/login has metadataStatus: fresh

scenarios.name: Specs listed per workspace with title
given: the project has workspace default with specs auth/login (title Login) and auth/register (title Register)
then: stdout shows default followed by rows containing default:auth/login  Login and default:auth/register  Register
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: Specs in lexicographic order within workspace
given: the default workspace contains specs z/spec, a/spec, and m/spec
then: the specs are listed in alphabetical order: default:a/spec, default:m/spec, default:z/spec

scenarios.name: JSON output without --summary
given: workspace default has spec auth/login with title Login
when: specd spec list --format json is run
then: stdout is valid JSON with workspaces[0].specs[0] having path equal to default:auth/login and title but no summary key

scenarios.name: JSON output with --summary and available summary
given: workspace default has spec auth/login with title Login and description Handles login
when: specd spec list --summary --format json is run
then: stdout is valid JSON with workspaces[0].specs[0] having path equal to default:auth/login, title, and summary equal to Handles login

scenarios.name: JSON output with --summary but no summary available
given: workspace default has spec auth/register with no extractable summary
when: specd spec list --summary --format json is run
then: the entry for default:auth/register has path and title but no summary key

scenarios.name: Workspace with no specs
given: the billing workspace exists but has no specs
then: stdout shows the billing heading followed by (none)

scenarios.name: No workspaces at all
given: specd.yaml has no workspaces configured
then: stdout contains no workspaces configured
AND the process exits with code 0
